title: verbascum rotundifolium
intro: Verbascum rotundifolium, also known as the round-leaved mullein, is a biennial plant that belongs to the figwort family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The plant's leaves and flowers contain mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful for relieving coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically to soothe skin irritations, such as burns or eczema.
